response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine staff-level depth across distributed systems, TypeScript, and — critically — hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work in his current role, which maps directly to the AI-native engineering environment described. His high-throughput systems experience at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests per second, async I/O, low-latency DLang services) is strong evidence for the distributed systems requirement. However, Python is entirely absent from his resume despite being a must-have technical skill at this seniority, which is a hard gap to overlook. Additionally, explicit evidence of cross-functional stakeholder management, on-call ownership, and incident-response experience is thin. Worth a screening call to probe the Python gap and operational readiness before committing to a full loop.
strengths: Current role involves direct hands-on work on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling for agentic workflows — an immediate fit for an AI-native engineering culture
Proven high-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async architectures

Consistent staff-level positioning across multiple employers with clear ownership behaviors: founding GISCollective, absorbing iOS ownership at WatchUp, and driving TDD/CI/CD engineering quality practices at RIDE Capital
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume despite being listed as a must-have skill — this is a significant gap for a Staff-level hire and cannot be assumed from adjacent experience
No explicit mention of on-call rotations, incident response, or operational ownership at scale — key expectations called out in the role requirements
Several tenures are short (6–15 months at Sociomantic, RIDE, NI), which may indicate limited exposure to the long-arc technical strategy and cross-stakeholder coordination expected of a Staff IC
